rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: The overall aim of this course is for students to acquire the knowledge that they need to succeed in the EIKEN Pre-1st test. This includes knowledge of the test format, and the the comprehension and productive skills on which they are tested. Students will also learn the techniques that are used to produce the test. Practical exercises in all areas will help to reinforce this knowledge.
aims: 英検準一級試験で合格するために必要な知識、戦略、スキル。
goals: Students will develop the ability to use the reading skills and strategies that are tested in EIKEN Pre-1st
Students will develop the ability to use the listening skills and strategies that are tested in EIKEN Pre-1st
Students will develop the ability to use the writing skills and strategies that are tested in EIKEN Pre-1st
Students will develop the ability to use the speaking skills and strategies that are tested in EIKEN Pre-1st
Students will work to acquire the academic vocabulary that is used in EIKEN Pre-1st
Students will develop a thorough understanding of the purposes of the EIKEN Pre-1st and other standardised English tests, and the techniques that are used to produce them.
textbooks: All materials for this course will be supplied by the instructor, and can be accessed via the MOODLE website.
prereq: Students registering for this course should have acquired (or have language proficiency equivalent to) EIKEN 2nd grade. They must also be prepared to commit to preparation outside of class.
prep: In order to succeed in the EIKEN Pre-1st test an examinee requires a good command of vocabuary. Students are therefore required to complete weekly online vocabulary study assignments, which will be part of their final grade. They must also complete other assignments, as required.

officeHour.name: Michael Sharpe

extra.text: Participants in this course are required to complete weekly online vocabulary study assignments in order to build the vocabulary knowledge required for the test, as well as in-class exercises. They will also have both a pre- and post assessment to measure progress.
